key_facts: Rheonics specializes in inline process density and viscosity monitoring.
The products displayed are inline sensors designed for process measurement.
Rheonics' products carry EHEDG, IECEx, ATEX, ISO 9001:2015, and CE certifications.
The EHEDG certification is Type EL Class I (ML 0000).
Rheonics maintains contact offices in Winterthur, Switzerland, and Houston, USA.
The document was published in July 2023.

chunks.page_summary: This page presents a comparison of the SRV and SRD inline viscometers and density sensors, detailing their specifications and applications for various industrial processes.
chunks.extracted_text:
Product Portfolio

SRV
Description: Process viscometer for Newtonian and non-Newtonian fluids. Wide viscosity range - monitor the complete process.

SRD
Description: Single instrument for process density, viscosity and temperature measurement.

Fluid Measurements
Viscosity Range
SRV: 3 to 10,000 cP
SRV (available): 0.5 to 50,000 cP
SRD: 1 to 3,000 cP
SRD (available): wider range available

Viscosity Accuracy
SRV (standard): 5% of reading
SRV (available): 1% & higher accuracy available
SRD (standard): 5% of reading
SRD (available): higher accuracy available

SRV: -
SRD: 0.0 - 4.0 g/cc

SRV: -

Reproducibility
SRV: Better than 0.1% of reading
SRD: Better than 0.1% of reading

Temperature (inbuilt)

SRD: Pt1000 (DIN EN 60751 class B)

Operational Environment

SRV: -40 up to 285 °C
SRD: -40 up to 285 °C

SRD: up to 10,000 psi (690 bar)

Material (Wetted parts)

SRD: 316L Stainless Steel, Hastelloy C22

SRD: Flush, Short and Long insertion

SRD: Threaded, Flange, Sanitary

SRD: IP69K

SRD: M12 (8-pin, A-coded)

Application
General: Designed for easy installation in pipelines, tanks, and process lines.

SRV Specific Applications:
• Process viscosity control of slurries, emulsions and other non-newtonian fluids
• Polymerization monitoring
• Coating and ink viscosity control
• Marine fuel viscosity control

SRD Specific Applications:
• Drilling mud density and viscosity
• Newtonian and nonnewtonian fluids
• Pipeline and pumping - efficiency and leak detection
• Fuel consumption monitoring

†subject to change without notice
key_facts: The SRV is a dedicated process viscometer for Newtonian and non-Newtonian fluids, offering a wide viscosity range.
The SRD is a versatile instrument capable of simultaneous process density, viscosity, and temperature measurement.
Both the SRV and SRD models achieve a high reproducibility of better than 0.1% of reading.
Both instruments feature an inbuilt Pt1000 temperature sensor, compliant with DIN EN 60751 class B.
The operational environment for both devices spans a wide process fluid temperature range of -40 to 285 °C and a pressure range up to 10,000 psi (690 bar).
Wetted parts for both SRV and SRD are constructed from 316L Stainless Steel and Hastelloy C22.
Both instruments are available with Flush, Short, and Long insertion variants and offer Threaded, Flange, or Sanitary process connections, with EHEDG certified hygienic options.
A robust IP69K ingress protection rating is provided for both the SRV and SRD.
Electrical connection for both devices is via an M12 (8-pin, A-coded) connector.
SRV applications include viscosity control for slurries, emulsions, non-Newtonian fluids, polymerization monitoring, coating/ink viscosity control, and marine fuel viscosity control.

SRD applications include drilling mud density and viscosity measurement, analysis of Newtonian and non-Newtonian fluids, pipeline and pumping efficiency/leak detection, and fuel consumption monitoring.
Both viscometers are designed for easy installation in pipelines, tanks, and general process lines.

key_facts: Rheonics offers inline process density and viscosity monitoring solutions.
The DVP sensor is designed for simultaneous density, viscosity, and temperature measurement at HPHT conditions.
The DVM sensor is specifically designed for reservoir fluid analysis and can measure density and viscosity at up to 30,000 psi and 200 °C.
Both DVP and DVM sensors offer options for higher accuracy in viscosity and density measurements.
The DVP and DVM sensors feature wetted parts made of Titanium Grade 5.
Rheonics electronics modules (SME-TRD and SME-DRM) support various analog and digital output protocols, including wireless Bluetooth LE 4.0.
Software for data acquisition and service is available via iOS and Android apps.
DVP applications include gas and liquid density, high-pressure processes, custody transfer for liquid and gas, and LNG density metering.
DVM applications include HPHT fluid analysis, PVT viscosity & density, EOR density & viscosity, core flow fluid measurements, and lubricant viscosity monitoring.
warnings_notes: DVP: Not suitable for liquids with magnetic particles.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides an overview of Rheonics' inline process density and viscosity monitoring solutions, highlighting their Swiss origin, technological advancements over 30 years, and global presence.
product_names: HPHT Viscosity and Density Sensor for Oil and Gas (DVM)

rheonics
inline process
density and viscosity
monitoring

INVENTED, DESIGNED, AND BUILT
WITH SWISS PRECISION

Invented, designed and built by an ETH Zurich spin-off team with over 150 years
of collective experience in resonant sensor technology. Rheonics proprietary
technology is protected by a growing portfolio of US & international patents.

Precision built in Switzerland, each Rheonics fluid density and viscosity sensor is
designed to match your application needs. Whether you need to measure density
and viscosity downhole at 30,000 psi and 200 °C or monitor the viscosity of
polymerization reactions, we have a solution for you.

Rheonics density and viscosity sensors are available in probe and flow through
styles. All Rheonics products are designed to withstand the harshest process
environments including high level of shock, vibrations, abrasives & corrosives.

key_facts: Rheonics was invented, designed, and built by an ETH Zurich spin-off team with over 150 years of collective experience in resonant sensor technology.
Rheonics' proprietary technology is protected by a growing portfolio of US & international patents.
Each Rheonics fluid density and viscosity sensor is precision built in Switzerland.
Rheonics density and viscosity sensors are available in probe and flow-through styles.
All Rheonics products are designed to withstand the harshest process environments, including high levels of shock, vibrations, abrasives, and corrosives.
Rheonics has been developing innovative fluid density and viscosity monitoring for 30 years, with its conceptual framework established at ETH Zurich in 1985.

Rheonics has received multiple awards, including the 2021 FTA Technical Innovation Award, 2021 Swiss Excellence Product Award, 2021 Gravure Award for Sustainable Packaging, and 2022 AIMCAL Technology of the Year Awards.
